codeReview.summary: Clean, minimal replay of the v6.9.31 HEAD /healthz support. The implementation extracts the existing inline healthz handler into a named closure, adds a method check for HEAD requests returning 200 with no body, and registers the handler on both GET and HEAD routes. Tests are properly subtest-structured, asserting unchanged GET JSON behavior and new HEAD empty-body 200 behavior. No regressions, no scope creep, no security issues.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The pr-replay-worker skill is written around PR-oriented workflows (gh pr view, gh pr diff) but this milestone replays a release commit rather than a PR. The worker had to adapt by using 'gh api repos/.../commits/<sha>' instead. The skill should document an explicit commit-inspection variant for release-tag milestones, as the worker noted in skillFeedback.suggestedChanges.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.deviations step 1: 'Used gh api to inspect commit 1716a845... instead of gh pr view/gh pr diff.' and skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'Document an explicit commit-inspection variant of the replay procedure for release-tag milestones.'

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker reported followedProcedure=false due to the commit-inspection deviation and the reviewer fallback deviation. Both deviations were justified given the environment constraints. The skill's procedure wording should be updated to make the commit-based path a first-class option rather than a deviation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.followedProcedure is false with two documented deviations, both with valid justification.

summary: PASS. The HEAD /healthz replay is correct, minimal, and well-tested. The implementation refactors the inline healthz handler into a shared closure that dispatches on HTTP method: HEAD returns 200 with empty body via c.Status(), GET preserves the existing JSON response. Both GET and HEAD routes are registered explicitly via s.engine.GET and s.engine.HEAD. The test refactors the flat TestHealthz into two subtests (GET and HEAD) asserting unchanged GET semantics (200 + JSON {"status":"ok"}) and new HEAD behavior (200 + empty body). Diff scope is exactly 2 files (server.go + server_test.go), matching the upstream commit surface. No security concerns: healthz remains unauthenticated with no sensitive data leakage. The handler uses c.Status() which correctly omits the body for HEAD per Gin/HTTP semantics. Two skill-level observations noted for the pr-replay-worker procedure (commit-based replay path and reviewer fallback should be documented as first-class options, not deviations).
